title: Abaya Pradhan Temple
tagline: Abaya Pradhan Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Thirumittakode Anchumoorthi Temple (also called Thiruviththuvakkōdu) is a Hindu temple equally associated with the deities Vishnu and Shiva.
overview.description: Thirumittakode Anchumoorthi Temple (also called Thiruviththuvakkōdu) is a Hindu temple equally associated with the deities Vishnu and Shiva. Temple is situated on the banks of the Bharathappuzha at Thirumittacode of Palakkad District in Kerala state in India. The five statues - one for Shiva and four Vishnu - are known here as Thirumattikodu five (Anchu) Moorthy Temple. As a Shiva temple, it is regarded as 108 Shivalayam of Shaivas.

topPlaces.name: Muthuthala
topPlaces.description: Muthuthala is a village and gram panchayat. It lies northwest of Pattambi in Palakkad district, of the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Pattambi
topPlaces.description: Pattambi is a town, tehsil, and municipality in the Palakkad District of Kerala, India. It is also the administrative headquarters of the Pattambi Taluk.

topPlaces.name: Kattil Madam Temple
topPlaces.description: Kattil Madam Temple is a dilapidated shrine in the Palakkad district in Kerala, India. It is thought to be a Jain temple built around the ninth or tenth centuries AD and is situated on the Pattambi Guruvayur road.

topPlaces.name: Arangottukara
topPlaces.description: Arangottukara is a small town in Thrissur district and Palakkad district in the state of Kerala, India on the border between palakkad and thrissur districts. India.

topPlaces.name: Vavanoor
topPlaces.description: Vavanoor is a village in Pattambi taluk in Palakkad district of Kerala state, south India. The village is located near Koottanad, Pattambi.

topPlaces.name: Pallur, Thrissur
topPlaces.description: Pallur is a village in Thrissur district in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Maruthur
topPlaces.description: Maruthur is a village in the Palayamkottai Block, Palayam kottai taluk Tirunelveli district of Tamil Nadu, in India.

topPlaces.name: Nagalassery
topPlaces.description: Nagalassery is a village and gram panchayat in Palakkad district in the state of Kerala, India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Abaya Pradhan Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Abaya Pradhan Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Abaya Pradhan Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Calicut International Airport, Kozhikode (47 km) and the nearest railway station is Coimbatore Junction (89 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Abaya Pradhan Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Abaya Pradhan Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Abaya Pradhan Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Abaya Pradhan Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Abaya Pradhan Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Muthuthala, Pattambi, Kattil Madam Temple, Arangottukara, Vavanoor.
